articles.headline: Iran President Ebrahim Raisi Dies in Helicopter Crash in Mountains
articles.summary: Iranian President Ebrahim Raisi and Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian were found dead at a helicopter crash site in East Azerbaijan province. The crash occurred during heavy fog. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei announced five days of mourning and named Vice President Mohammad Mokhber as acting president. New elections must be conducted within 50 days to appoint a successor.

articles.headline: Singapore Airlines Flight Turbulence Leaves One Dead and Thirty Injured
articles.summary: A 73-year-old British man died and 30 others were injured when a Singapore Airlines flight from London encountered severe turbulence over the Irrawaddy Basin. The Boeing 777-300ER dropped 6,000 feet in minutes, throwing unbuckled passengers into the ceiling. The aircraft made an emergency landing in Bangkok. Passengers described the experience as a sudden drop with no warning to strap in.

articles.headline: Pakistan willing to host US-Iran peace talks
articles.summary: Pakistan's Prime Minister expressed willingness to host US-Iran peace talks, a day after President Trump postponed bombing threats, citing "productive" discussions. The US is deploying thousands of troops to the Middle East, fueling conflict fears.

articles.headline: Probe into cockpit audios, controllers after plane crash
articles.summary: Investigators are reviewing cockpit voice recorder and control tower communications for a collision between an Air Canada jet and a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport. The crash killed two pilots and injured several passengers.

articles.headline: 'No permission needed to navigate Strait of Hormuz'
articles.summary: A senior government official stated no country's permission is needed to sail through the Strait of Hormuz, rejecting claims of Indian vessels being stranded. Movement is covered by international navigation conventions.

articles.headline: Bahrain supports UNSC action for Hormuz transit
articles.summary: Bahrain proposed a draft UN Security Council resolution allowing countries to use "all necessary means" to protect commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The move addresses growing concerns over Iran threatening the strategic chokepoint.

articles.headline: US paper questions core WTO principles ahead of key meet
articles.summary: A US paper on WTO reforms questions MFN, special treatment for developing countries, and consensus-based decision-making. Released before the 14th Ministerial Conference, it pushes for plurilateral agreements, increasing pressure on India to reconsider its resistance to integrating Investment Facilitation for Development.

articles.headline: Pakistan army chief emerges as unlikely peacemaker in West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Pakistan army chief Field Marshal Asim Munir has emerged as a potential mediator to halt missile strikes between Israel and Iran, despite complex geopolitical dynamics and past US actions. The conflict, rooted in decades of mistrust over nuclear ambitions and regional proxies, is impacting global financial markets and oil prices, raising questions about Munir's effectiveness.

articles.headline: Trump, Modi Discuss Hormuz Strait Opening Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump and PM Narendra Modi discussed the importance of keeping the Strait of Hormuz open for global trade, through which a fifth of global energy supplies pass. India supports de-escalation and restoration of peace at the earliest for regional stability.

articles.headline: US Media Reports JD Vance May Lead US-Iran Talks in Pakistan
articles.summary: US media outlet Axios reported that Vice President JD Vance might be appointed as the chief negotiator for potential US-Iran talks in Pakistan. This follows President Trump's statements about 'productive' negotiations to de-escalate the West Asia crisis, aiming for peace and stability.

articles.headline: US Under Secy: US & India Don't Need Full Agreement for Cooperation
articles.summary: US Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby stated that the US and India don't need to agree on everything to cooperate effectively, especially for a favorable Indo-Pacific power balance. He supports India's efforts to build a strong, indigenous defence industrial base, enhancing sovereignty.

articles.headline: India Clarifies No Permission Needed for Hormuz Strait Transit
articles.summary: India clarified that no permission is required for ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz, reiterating it's an international strait with freedom of navigation. This follows reports of Iranian clearance requirements, though movements must be assessed for safety and appropriate timing.

articles.headline: Iran and Israel Exchange Blows Despite Trump's Peace Efforts
articles.summary: Airstrikes battered Iran, and Iranian missiles targeted Israel, a day after US President Trump spoke of "productive" talks to halt the West Asia war. Iran denied negotiations, while Israeli officials saw a deal as unlikely, as conflict continued in the Gulf and Lebanon with fresh attacks.

articles.headline: Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr Named Head of Iran's National Security Council
articles.summary: Iran appointed former Revolutionary Guards commander Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as head of its Supreme National Security Council, succeeding Ali Larijani, who was killed in an Israeli strike. Zolghadr's background suggests a major role in Iran's war effort against US and Israeli strikes.

articles.headline: World Economic Forum Postpones Jeddah Conference Amid Iran War
articles.summary: The World Economic Forum deferred its Global Collaboration and Growth Meeting in Jeddah, Saudi Arabia, due to the Iran war. This is the latest high-profile event affected by the regional conflict, following similar postponements by private equity firms, ensuring full strategic impact.

articles.headline: Saudi Prince Urges Trump to Continue War Against Iran
articles.summary: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman has reportedly urged US President Donald Trump to continue the war against Iran, seeing it as a "historic opportunity" to remake West Asia by destroying Iran's hardline government, despite Iranian drone and missile attacks disrupting oil markets.

articles.headline: Lufthansa, Cathay Pacific Extend Flight Suspensions in Middle East
articles.summary: Lufthansa and Cathay Pacific extended flight suspensions to Middle East destinations like Tehran, Beirut, and Riyadh until end of April/May due to the "volatile situation" caused by the war. Lufthansa's services to Dubai and Tel Aviv are cancelled until May 31, affecting travel.

articles.headline: Airstrikes Hit Iran-backed Militia HQ in Iraq, Killing 15
articles.summary: Airstrikes hit a headquarters of Iraq’s Iran-backed Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF) and a residence of its leader in Anbar province, killing at least 15 fighters, including the PMF's operations commander. This marks an escalation in US-Israeli strikes on Tehran's main regional allies, with the death toll expected to rise.

articles.headline: French Nuclear Power Helps Stabilize Europe's Energy Prices
articles.summary: France is achieving its highest level of nuclear energy generation since 2019, helping support power markets across Europe. This shields Europe from sharp natural gas price rises caused by the West Asia conflict, with French reactors nearly doubling power exports, covering demand from neighboring markets.

articles.headline: Pune Minor Granted Bail with Essay Condition After Fatal Porsche Crash
articles.summary: A 17-year-old was granted bail hours after crashing a Porsche into a motorcycle, killing two engineers in Pune. The Juvenile Justice Board's conditions, including writing a 300-word essay on road accidents, sparked nationwide outrage. Police are now moving a higher court to try the minor as an adult, citing the heinous nature of the crime and evidence of intoxication before the incident.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reserves Order on Patanjali Misleading Advertisements Case
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has reserved its judgment on the contempt petition against Baba Ramdev and Patanjali Ayurved. While the court noted the public apologies, it criticized the Uttarakhand State Licensing Authority for failing to act against the firm for years. The bench emphasized that the rule of law must be upheld regardless of the status of the individuals or companies involved.

articles.headline: Formula E case: KTR among five charged in Hyderabad
articles.summary: Telangana's anti-corruption bureau filed a charge sheet in the Formula E race case, naming Bharat Rashtra Samithi (BRS) working president K T Rama Rao as the primary accused among five for alleged misappropriation of ₹54.88 crore.

articles.headline: HC to govt: Address security issue for judicial officers
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court directed the Centre, Delhi government, and police to meet within a week to address providing personal security officers and ensuring adequate security at judicial officers' residences, citing the safety of trial court judges as a significant concern.

articles.headline: SC says election timing no bar in I-PAC raids trial
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that election timing is not a barrier for hearing ED's plea accusing West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee of obstructing a money laundering probe into I-PAC's office. The court emphasized upholding rule of law, not influencing elections.

articles.headline: Gujarat House passes UCC Bill amid Oppn protests
articles.summary: The Gujarat assembly passed the Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026, making it the second state to enact a common legal framework for all religions on marriage, divorce, inheritance, and adoption. The bill makes registration of relationships mandatory and bars multiple spouses.

articles.headline: HC dismisses Lalu's plea to quash CBI FIR
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court dismissed RJD chief Lalu Prasad's plea to quash a CBI FIR in the alleged land-for-jobs scam, ruling that Section 17A of the Prevention of Corruption Act, introduced in 2018, does not apply to offenses committed between 2004-09.

articles.headline: Transgender Bill passed in LS amid opposition
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill 2026. The bill amends the 2019 Act, redefining transgender, removing self-perceived gender identity, and excluding certain socio-cultural identities, drawing criticism for being "unconstitutional" and lacking community consultation.

articles.headline: SC: Exclusive NIA courts for states with over 10 trials
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed states with over 10 NIA trials to establish exclusive NIA courts as a pilot project with Central funding, aiming for speedy trials under the National Investigation Agency Act and UAPA.

articles.headline: Zero FIR in B'luru on Rohit's 'conspiracy' charge in Pawar death
articles.summary: Bengaluru police registered a Zero FIR on Rohit Pawar's complaint alleging a "larger criminal conspiracy" in the death of former Maharashtra deputy CM Ajit Pawar in a plane crash. Rohit claims "systematic violations" of aviation safety regulations led to the incident.

articles.headline: Caste and the Indian reality of exclusion
articles.summary: This opinion piece discusses the Supreme Court's ruling on losing SC status upon conversion, arguing that caste transcends religion in India. It highlights the persistence of caste-based discrimination in Christianity and Islam, suggesting the ruling contradicts the right to religion for SC converts.

articles.headline: Jury finds Cosby sexually assaulted woman, awards $59mn
articles.summary: A civil jury in California found Bill Cosby liable for drugging and sexually assaulting Donna Motsinger in 1972, awarding her $59.25 million in damages. Cosby's attorney plans to appeal the verdict.

articles.headline: NCLAT denies interim stay on Vedanta plea against Adani's JAL bid
articles.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) declined to grant an interim stay on Vedanta Group's plea against the NCLT's approval of Adani Group's ₹14,535 crore bid for acquiring Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL). The NCLAT sought a response from JAL's Committee of Creditors in a week.

articles.headline: ED detects ₹36,000 crore proceeds across 257 cybercrime cases
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) has identified proceeds of crime totaling nearly ₹36,000 crore in 257 cybercrime cases investigated until February 28. The agency coordinates closely with other law enforcement bodies and leverages platforms like SAHYOG and the cyber police portal for information sharing and tracking financial irregularities.

articles.headline: No non-Hindu, Buddhist, Sikh can claim Scheduled Caste status: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that individuals professing religions other than Hinduism, Buddhism, or Sikhism cannot claim Scheduled Caste status, affirming Clause 3 of the 1950 order. Conversion to another religion results in immediate and complete loss of SC status, irrespective of birth.

articles.headline: Presumption about short careers hurting women officers, says SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that systemic bias, including the presumption of short careers for women officers, led to an uneven playing field, crippling their chances for permanent commission in the armed forces. It upheld women officers’ rights to equal opportunity and dignity, stating vacancy caps cannot override constitutional obligations.

articles.headline: SC Flags Disparity in Evaluation of Women SSCOs, Grants Relief
articles.summary: The Supreme Court highlighted systemic disparity in evaluating women Short Service Commission Officers across armed forces. It ruled that historical assumptions about women's limited career prospects led to an uneven playing field, hindering their permanent commission chances, and granted corrective relief including pensionary benefits.

articles.headline: SC Directs States to Identify Courts for One-Year UAPA Trial Completion
articles.summary: The Supreme Court directed all states and UTs to identify and report the number of exclusive courts needed to complete UAPA trials within one year on a day-to-day basis. It also called for notifying special public prosecutors and adequate court staff in consultation with high courts.

articles.headline: Changes to Companies, LLPs Law Signal Shift to Facilitation: Experts
articles.summary: Proposed amendments to laws governing companies and limited liability partnerships indicate India's move towards a modern, facilitation-focused regulatory framework. Experts note that implementation details regarding share buybacks, CSR exemptions, and audit relief will follow Parliament's final bill.

articles.headline: Sebi Allows WhatsApp Trade Instructions with Verifiable Records
articles.summary: Sebi will allow stock brokers to accept WhatsApp instructions for buying/selling shares, provided messages can be salvaged for investigation. This informal guidance requires brokers to establish verifiability of such communication and comply with electronic evidence laws, aiming to avoid disputes and unauthorized trades.

articles.headline: Phase Five of Lok Sabha Polls Records Over 57 Percent Turnout
articles.summary: The fifth phase of the general elections covering 49 constituencies across eight states saw an approximate turnout of 57.5%. Key candidates included Rahul Gandhi in Rae Bareli and Smriti Irani in Amethi. While polling was largely peaceful in states like Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh, sporadic incidents of violence and clashes between party workers were reported in parts of West Bengal.

articles.headline: Delhi Court Remands Kejriwal Aide Bibhav Kumar to Five-Day Custody
articles.summary: A Delhi court sent Bibhav Kumar, personal aide to CM Arvind Kejriwal, to five days in police custody regarding the alleged assault of AAP MP Swati Maliwal. Police claimed Kumar formatted his phone to hide evidence and remained uncooperative during questioning. The defense argued the arrest was a political move to target the AAP leadership during the critical final phases of the election.

articles.headline: 5-6 month delay in MGNREGA, panchayat polls: HC told
articles.summary: Karnataka's gram panchayat polls face a 5-6 month delay due to preparatory processes, including finalising member numbers based on census data and assigning reservations. This follows the assembly's decision to use paper ballots, which the opposition criticized.

articles.headline: Karnataka's ₹10-cr grant for Wayanad landslide relief sparks row
articles.summary: Karnataka's government allocated an additional ₹10 crore for landslide relief in Kerala's Wayanad, drawing BJP criticism over timing near Kerala assembly elections. CM Siddaramaiah defended it as a humanitarian decision in a federal system.

articles.headline: Karnataka cancels Upadhyaya lecture series at Mysuru varsity
articles.summary: Karnataka government cancelled a proposed three-day lecture series on RSS ideologue Deendayal Upadhyaya at KSOU, Mysuru, citing protocol violations. The decision followed scrutiny over the university's plan to host the event without prior intimation.

articles.headline: BJP chief's affidavit: Cong, CPI(M) plan legal action
articles.summary: Congress and CPI(M) plan legal action against Kerala BJP chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar over alleged concealment of a residential property in his election affidavit. Chandrasekhar called the allegations "100% false."

articles.headline: 'Hindu MLA' remark: CEO sends report to ECI
articles.summary: Kerala CEO Rathan U Kelkar received a legal opinion on BJP candidate B Gopalakrishnan's "Hindu MLA" remark and will send a report to the Election Commission. Gopalakrishnan allegedly claimed a constituency had not elected a Hindu MLA in decades.

articles.headline: ₹11.21 lakh spent on Kerala CM's interview; Oppn criticises
articles.summary: The Kerala government spent ₹11.21 lakh on an interview of CM Pinarayi Vijayan by actor Mohanlal, according to an RTI reply. The opposition criticized the use of taxpayer money for a political campaign, demanding the CM return the funds.

articles.headline: Senior Hyderabad Congress leader likely to quit party
articles.summary: Senior Congress leader TKS Jeevan Reddy is likely to quit the party on March 25, citing being sidelined in the Jagtial Assembly Constituency affairs despite 40 years of service.

articles.headline: CPI(M)'s Kerala assembly polls bid faces challenges
articles.summary: CPI(M) faces challenges in Kerala assembly polls, including rebel candidates in four strongholds and potential anti-incumbency after a decade in power. Rebels, like G Sudhakaran and V Kunhikrishnan, are backed by the Congress-led UDF.

articles.headline: AIADMK manifesto: ₹2,000 monthly to female heads
articles.summary: AIADMK general secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami released the party's manifesto, promising ₹2,000 monthly to female heads of households, ₹10,000 special assistance per family, free lentils and refrigerators, and free bus travel for men.

articles.headline: Discuss amendment bill on delimitation: CM Stalin
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM M K Stalin raised concerns about women's representation based on the 2011 Census, urging a special Parliament session in June to discuss constitutional amendments for delimitation, increased seats, and states' proportional representation.

articles.headline: Defence minister: Learn from West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Defence minister Rajnath Singh urged military brass to draw "operational and technological" lessons from the US-Israel war with Iran to bolster India’s defence preparedness. He stressed self-reliance and operational readiness, noting recent global conflicts question "short and decisive wars."

articles.headline: Mamata banks on loyalists in Bhabanipur battleground
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee is relying on trusted lieutenants to campaign in her Bhabanipur constituency, while she focuses on other parts of the state. BJP has fielded Suvendu Adhikari against her in the April 29 polls.

articles.headline: Bengal CM kicks off campaign from Darjeeling
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee started her campaign from Darjeeling's Himalayan foothills, addressing voters. She emphasized Bengal's inclusiveness. Abhishek Banerjee campaigned in South 24 Parganas for the April 23 and 29 assembly elections.

articles.headline: Kerala Police asks X to remove posts on EC doc with BJP seal
articles.summary: Kerala Police issued notices to social media users and X to remove posts related to an ECI document carrying the BJP seal, which the state CEO called a "clerical error." The CEO suspended the responsible officer, but CM Mamata Banerjee called it a "political conspiracy."

articles.headline: Fish on menu in TMC, BJP Bengal poll campaigns
articles.summary: Both TMC and BJP in West Bengal are incorporating fish into their poll campaigns to win over Bengali voters. This comes after TMC alleged BJP is a "party of outsiders" against Bengal's culture, prompting BJP candidates to highlight their non-vegetarian habits.

articles.headline: Confusion surrounds 1st supplementary voters list in Bengal
articles.summary: Confusion surrounds the publication of West Bengal's first supplementary electoral roll, with ECI admitting no data on deletions post-adjudication. CM Mamata Banerjee criticized the timing and lack of transparency.

articles.headline: SC: Electoral roll revision smooth, except in Bengal
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed that the special intensive revision (SIR) of electoral rolls proceeded smoothly in all states except West Bengal. The bench highlighted a category of "logical discrepancy" uniquely introduced in the state, affecting 13.6 million voters.

articles.headline: MHA: No separate ministry for Union Territories
articles.summary: The Ministry of Home Affairs informed Parliament that there is no proposal to create a dedicated ministry, parliamentary inspection committee, or special policy framework for Union Territories, stating existing mechanisms are sufficient.

articles.headline: CAPF bill listed, 8 hours allotted in Rajya Sabha
articles.summary: The Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, is listed for introduction in the Rajya Sabha by Home Minister Amit Shah, with 8 hours allotted for discussion. The bill aims to unify CAPFs' legal framework and codify IPS officer deputation at senior levels.

articles.headline: Nitish elected unopposed as JD(U) president
articles.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar was elected unopposed as the JD(U) national president, as no other candidate filed nomination for the party's top post. He had taken over in 2023.

articles.headline: Sonia Gandhi hospitalised due to weather change
articles.summary: Congress chief Parliamentary Party chairperson Sonia Gandhi was admitted to Sir Ganga Ram Hospital in Delhi on Tuesday, feeling unwell due to the change in weather. She is under observation, but her condition is not serious.

articles.headline: Amritpal Singh seeks condonation for LS absence
articles.summary: Incarcerated MP Amritpal Singh wrote to the Lok Sabha Speaker requesting condonation for his absence from Parliament. This follows information to the high court that his absence reached almost 60 days, risking his seat.

articles.headline: Why US and Iran may exit a costly war
articles.summary: This opinion piece suggests US President Trump's announcement of talks with Iran reflects a need to end the costly West Asia war due to rising oil prices and domestic economic pressures. Iran, despite defiant posturing, is also suffering from dwindling resources and public morale.

articles.headline: Who is the US dealing with in Iran?
articles.summary: The article discusses the unclear leadership structure in Tehran after recent killings, making US negotiations with Iran difficult. Key figures like Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf (Parliament Speaker) and Mohammad Masoud Pezeshkian (President) are mentioned as potential points of contact.

articles.headline: Ex-Guards commander to succeed slain security chief
articles.summary: Iran named former Revolutionary Guards commander Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr to replace Ali Larijani as head of the Supreme National Security Council (SNSC). Zolghadr, a veteran insider, will play a major role in Iran's war effort at a critical juncture.

articles.headline: Outgoing PM tipped to Danish parl election
articles.summary: Danes voted in a general election where Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen is favored after her strong stance against US President Donald Trump over Greenland.

articles.headline: PM Modi forms 7 groups to tackle fuel, fertilizer supply disruptions
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi established seven Empowered Groups of Secretary-level officers to address global energy shock, safeguard fuel/fertilizer supplies, and manage trade flow disruptions from the West Asia war. These groups will identify risks, resolve supply issues, and control price volatility, chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh.

articles.headline: AIADMK unveils 297 poll promises ahead of Tamil Nadu elections
articles.summary: AIADMK unveiled 297 poll promises for Tamil Nadu elections, including ₹10,000 special assistance per family to offset rising prices, ₹25,000 subsidy for 5 lakh women on two-wheelers, and three free gas cylinders annually for rice cardholders. The party aims to reduce financial burden and boost living standards.

articles.headline: VD Satheesan predicts 100+ seats for Team UDF in Kerala polls
articles.summary: Opposition leader VD Satheesan expressed confidence that the UDF would secure over 100 seats in Kerala Assembly elections, citing strong anti-incumbency against the ruling LDF and decisive majority in recent civic body polls. He pledged welfare programs if elected, accusing the government of corruption.

articles.headline: Bhabanipur set for high-stakes Mamata-Adhikari showdown in polls
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee will face arch-rival Suvendu Adhikari in Bhabanipur for next month's Assembly elections. Both parties are intensifying campaigning, with BJP confident of overturning Trinamool Congress' Lok Sabha lead due to anti-incumbency and corruption allegations, while Banerjee relies on local support and welfare schemes.

articles.headline: Rajeev Chandrasekhar’s nomination cleared after asset disclosure concerns
articles.summary: Kerala BJP State President Rajeev Chandrasekhar's nomination for the Nemom constituency was accepted after initial objections regarding non-disclosure of assets were resolved. The Congress had alleged improper declaration of a Bengaluru property, but election authorities cleared the nomination after reviewing the affidavit.

articles.headline: Twenty20 partners with NDA to rewrite Kerala’s electoral playbook
articles.summary: Business-backed outfit Twenty20, led by Kitex Garments MD Sabu M Jacob, has joined the BJP-led NDA to contest 20 seats in Kerala. This aims to disrupt the LDF-UDF dominance, appealing to disillusioned voters seeking efficiency and accountability. Jacob highlights Kerala's economic lag compared to NDA-ruled states.

articles.headline: Charting the BJP’s electoral ascent in Kerala’s capital
articles.summary: Thiruvananthapuram exhibits historical affinity with Hindu political mobilization, influenced by temple institutions and caste alignments. This translated into electoral opportunities for BJP-aligned groups, especially after the 1980s Chalai market riots. Veteran O Rajagopal's 2016 Nemom victory marked a breakthrough, challenging the district's Left-leaning vote.

articles.headline: Oppn calls for all-party meet on govt’s 33% women’s reservation plan
articles.summary: Opposition parties, including Congress and DMK, demanded an all-party meeting to discuss the government's plan to amend the Nari Vandan Adhiniyam, 2023, for 33% women's reservation in Parliament by 2029 polls. Concerns include using the 2011 Census for delimitation and the timing of the announcement.

articles.headline: Kerala parties mull chartering flights to bring Gulf voters
articles.summary: With Kerala's Assembly elections tight, political parties are considering chartering flights to bring home 2.23 lakh overseas voters from Gulf nations. Flight cuts and prohibitive fares threaten voter turnout, especially for the working class. Parties like the Muslim League and Congress are actively exploring these logistics.

articles.headline: PM Modi Seeks States' Support to Tackle West Asia Crisis Economic Impact
articles.summary: PM Modi urged states to cooperate in addressing the economic impact of the West Asia crisis, emphasizing relief for the poor and migrants, and essential supply. He stressed working as 'Team India' to maintain rapid growth, recalling the Covid crisis response, as the conflict may last long.

articles.headline: Cabinet Secretariat Forms 7 Groups to Deal With Gulf Crisis
articles.summary: The Cabinet Secretariat established seven Groups of Secretaries (GoS) comprising 60-65 senior officials for coordinated response to the West Asia crisis. These groups focus on security, economy, energy, logistics, essential commodities, and public outreach, signalling a mission-mode approach.

articles.headline: Kerala Police Order Takedown of EC Letter with BJP Seal Posts
articles.summary: Kerala Police directed X handles to remove posts sharing an Election Commission communication bearing a BJP seal. The EC clarified it inadvertently attached the party's letter. Police deemed the content "blatantly insulting" to a national institution and a risk to public order by potentially inciting division.

articles.headline: Trump Expresses Love for Elvis Presley During Graceland Visit
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump toured Elvis Presley's Graceland estate, expressing his admiration for the King of Rock and Roll. He commented on Presley's design choices and personal life, and called signing a replica guitar a "big honour" during his visit to Memphis.

articles.headline: IMD Issues Red Alert as Severe Heatwave Grips Northern India
articles.summary: The India Meteorological Department issued a red alert for Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana, and Delhi as temperatures surged past 47°C. Najafgarh in Delhi recorded a peak of 47.4°C, the highest in the country. The severe heatwave is expected to persist for several days due to dry westerly winds. Citizens are advised to avoid sun exposure and stay hydrated to prevent heat-related illnesses.

articles.headline: Loni is world's most polluted city; Delhi ranks fourth
articles.summary: Loni in Uttar Pradesh is ranked as the world's most polluted city with an annual average PM2.5 level of 112.5µg/m3. Delhi ranks fourth globally, according to the World Air Quality Report 2025.

articles.headline: Temporary forest staff in Karnataka call strike from April 1
articles.summary: Karnataka's State Forest Department Daily Wage Workers’ Association announced an indefinite strike from April 1, citing delayed wages and unmet assurances. The strike could disrupt operations in key reserves like Nagarahole and Bandipur, especially during fire season.

articles.headline: KKR Faces Sunrisers Hyderabad in High-Stakes IPL Qualifier One Today
articles.summary: Table-toppers Kolkata Knight Riders are set to play Sunrisers Hyderabad in the first qualifier of IPL 2024 at Ahmedabad. The winner will earn a direct ticket to the final. KKR relies on their balanced bowling attack led by Varun Chakaravarthy, while SRH will count on the explosive opening partnership of Travis Head and Abhishek Sharma to dominate the powerplay on a batting-friendly pitch.

articles.headline: Akshay Bhatia happy to play Hero Indian Open
articles.summary: Golfer Akshay Bhatia, fresh from winning the Arnold Palmer Invitational, is excited to play the Hero Indian Open, his first event in the country of his parents' birth. He discussed his resilience in tournaments and interactions with Tiger Woods.

articles.headline: Rinku Singh named KKR vice-captain
articles.summary: KKR named batter Rinku Singh as their vice-captain, assisting skipper Ajinkya Rahane, who was retained despite the team's poor 2025 campaign.

articles.headline: DC's Ben Duckett pulls out of IPL, faces ban
articles.summary: DC's Ben Duckett pulled out of the IPL, now facing a two-year ban, as he wants to play red-ball cricket. He was bought by DC for ₹2 crore.

articles.headline: Valverde removed as India women's football coach
articles.summary: AIFF’s technical committee recommended removing Amelia Valverde as head coach of the Indian women's football team.

articles.headline: Lakshya Sen to lead India's Thomas Cup squad
articles.summary: Lakshya Sen will spearhead India's challenge in the Thomas Cup. PV Sindhu and Unnati Hooda will lead the women's team in the Uber Cup next month.

articles.headline: Khelo India Tribal Games to revolutionise sports
articles.summary: The Khelo India Tribal Games, launching today, will provide a structured national platform for tribal athletes, who often lack access to facilities, coaching, and platforms. Talent identification teams will select promising players for advanced training at SAI centers.

articles.headline: New vibe as Indoor Athletics Nationals kicks-off
articles.summary: India's first domestic indoor athletics meet kicked off in Bhubaneswar, the future host of 2028 World Indoor Championships. Athletes faced new challenges with shorter 200m tracks and sharper bends. Pole vaulter Baranica Elangovan set a new national record of 4.22m.

articles.headline: Kashvee, Anushka called up for T20I series vs SA
articles.summary: All-rounder Kashvee Gautam and batter Anushka Sharma have been selected for India's T20I squad for the five-match series in South Africa, marking a significant progression for both players in the team's plans.

articles.headline: Chinnaswamy to honour 11 fans with empty seats
articles.summary: As a tribute to the 11 fans who died in a stampede at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium during RCB's IPL celebrations in 2025, the franchise decided to leave 11 seats empty for all future matches. Security measures are enhanced for public safety.

articles.headline: IPL Franchise Valuations Soar; RCB Sold for $1.8 Billion
articles.summary: A consortium including Aditya Birla Group, Times of India Group, Bolt Ventures, and Blackstone acquired Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) for $1.78 billion. Rajasthan Royals was valued at $1.6 billion. This sets new benchmarks for T20 franchises, marking a significant jump from original bid values, driven by fan engagement.

articles.headline: IPL Media Rights Auction Expected to See Limited Upside in Next Cycle
articles.summary: Media Partners Asia (MPA) projects IPL media rights for the 2028–32 cycle at $5.4 billion, largely in line with the current $6.2 billion, but a 13% decline in per-match value. This is due to monetisation plateaus, reduced competitive intensity, and a shift to free streaming, potentially leading to losses for rights holders.

articles.headline: Indian Benchmark Indices Close Lower Amid Global and Domestic Cues
articles.summary: Equity benchmarks Sensex and Nifty ended Tuesday in the red as investors remained cautious during the ongoing election cycle. The Sensex fell 52 points to 73,953, while the Nifty dropped 27 points to 22,502. Market volatility was fueled by continued selling from foreign institutional investors. Gains in the power and metal sectors were offset by losses in IT and pharmaceutical stocks.

articles.headline: 'Oil trading spiked before Trump's Iran remarks'
articles.summary: Thousands of oil contracts traded minutes before President Trump announced halting Iran strikes, causing prices to tumble. Analysts question if market participants acted on prior information, suggesting potential insider trading.

articles.headline: NCLAT clears way for Adani to take over JAL
articles.summary: The NCLAT refused to stay Adani Enterprises' acquisition of Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL), but clarified that all resolution steps remain subject to its final decision on Vedanta's appeal. It also refrained from preventing JAL's delisting.

articles.headline: Trump's Iran pause sparks Street relief
articles.summary: Indian markets rebounded sharply after US President Donald Trump announced a temporary halt to strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure, easing conflict concerns. Nifty 50 rose 1.78% and Sensex 30 rose 1.89%, recouping losses.

articles.headline: Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil
articles.summary: Reliance Industries bought 5 million barrels of Iranian crude days after the US temporarily waived sanctions on Iranian oil purchases. The oil was bought from the National Iranian Oil Company (NIOC) at a premium.

articles.headline: India media & entertainment sector to hit ₹3.3 lakh cr by 2028
articles.summary: India’s media and entertainment sector grew 9.1% to ₹2.78 lakh crore in 2025 and is projected to grow 7% annually to ₹3.3 lakh crore by 2028, driven largely by digital. Digital advertising rose 26% to ₹94,700 crore.

articles.headline: RCB valued at $1.78 b, Rajasthan Royals at $1.63 b in record IPL bids
articles.summary: IPL franchises Royal Challengers Bengaluru (acquired by Aditya Birla Group, Times Group, Bolt Ventures, Blackstone for $1.78 billion) and Rajasthan Royals (set to be sold to Kal Somani-led consortium backed by Walmart family for $1.63 billion) attracted record bids, setting new benchmarks for franchise valuations.

articles.headline: Steel companies gearing up to hike prices further
articles.summary: Indian steel companies anticipate a substantial price hike of ₹1,000–2,000 a tonne due to the West Asia war, which has spiked imported raw material and logistics costs. Domestic hot-rolled coil prices are already up 23%, but further rises may face resistance from price-sensitive sectors.

articles.headline: Jindal Stainless sets up 1.2 mtpa Indonesia plant
articles.summary: Jindal Stainless commissioned a 1.2 million tonnes per annum (mtpa) stainless steel melt shop (SMS) in Indonesia through a joint venture, ahead of schedule. This increases its total melting capacity to 4.2 mtpa. The company is also expanding facilities in Odisha and Hisar.

articles.headline: Priya Kapur removes mother-in-law Rani Kapur as RK Family trustee
articles.summary: Priya Kapur, trustee and beneficiary of the RK Family Trust, issued a notice removing her mother-in-law Rani Kapur as a trustee, citing breach of fiduciary duties. This follows Rani Kapur's civil suit disputing the trust's validity and claiming personal ownership of its assets after Sunjay Kapur's demise.

articles.headline: Maruti approves ₹10,189 crore investment for 2.5 lakh units capacity
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki's board approved ₹10,189 crore investment for the first phase of a car manufacturing plant in Khoraj Industrial Estate, Gujarat, adding an annual capacity of 2,50,000 units by 2029. This is driven by growth in domestic demand and exports, with existing plants operating above 100% capacity.

articles.headline: Bridgestone India to raise passenger car tyre capacity by 20%
articles.summary: Bridgestone India plans a ₹700–740 crore investment to expand its passenger car tyre capacity by over 20%, from 10.8 million to 12.8–13 million units over the next five years. This aligns with sustained growth in India’s automotive market, focusing on premium offerings and OEM partnerships.

articles.headline: Larger stake offer, better pricing can aid IDBI Bank sale
articles.summary: Reports suggest IDBI Bank's strategic sale failed due to bids falling short of the reserve price. The Centre should consider a more reasonable price and offer full control to buyers, potentially by offloading its and LIC's entire stake. Increasing floating stock could improve price discovery and prevent market distortion.

articles.headline: Nikon India eyes ₹1,000 crore revenue in FY26, 8% growth
articles.summary: Nikon India aims to achieve ₹1,000 crore in revenue for FY26, projecting an 8% growth from the previous fiscal. The company expects steady growth in FY27, driven by increased demand for photography and videography products from the rising creator economy. Nikon holds about 30% mirrorless camera market share.

articles.headline: BEL, RRP Group ink pact for advanced defence tech business
articles.summary: Navratna defence PSU Bharat Electronics Ltd (BEL) signed an MoU with the RRP Group to jointly explore business opportunities in advanced defence and technology domains. RRP Group, specializing in semiconductor manufacturing and aerospace/defence systems, will collaborate with BEL on these ventures.

articles.headline: J&K Bank gets ₹200 crore GST liability demand order
articles.summary: Jammu and Kashmir Bank received a demand order for ₹200.20 crore in GST liability from the Additional Commissioner, Central GST Commissionerate, Jammu. The bank maintains a strong case against the demand, believing it is without legal justification and will not materially impact its financials.

articles.headline: Nutraceutical market set for exponential growth, says Synthite’s Viju Jacob
articles.summary: India’s nutraceuticals and food supplements sector is projected to reach ₹1.1 lakh crore by 2030, driven by rising health awareness. Synthite Industries, with its NatXtra brand, targets ₹400 crore turnover in two years, focusing on gut health, PCOS, and gummy supplements, and expanding globally.

articles.headline: NTPC Green inks MoU with Nxtra Data for renewable energy supply
articles.summary: NTPC Green Energy Ltd (NGEL) signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Nxtra Data Ltd to develop renewable energy projects. This partnership aims to supply round-the-clock (RTC) power to Nxtra’s data centers across India, meeting their captive power requirements on a pan-India basis.

articles.headline: Narayana Health Insurance widens Aditi footprint to six new markets
articles.summary: Narayana Health Insurance is expanding its low-cost, hospital-linked 'Aditi' health plan to Guwahati, Dharwad, Kolar, Jaipur, Jamshedpur, and Davanagere. This move aims to provide integrated care, affordability, and access to over 2,400 hospitals nationwide, particularly for India’s 'missing middle' population.

articles.headline: Nova IVF enters Kerala with Craft Fertility acquisition, plans 10 new centres
articles.summary: Nova IVF Fertility, backed by Asia Healthcare Holdings (AHH), acquired Craft Fertility, marking its entry into Kerala. The partnership plans to launch 10 new IVF centres across the state in 3-5 years, bringing advanced reproductive medicine and AI-based embryo selection technology to address the region's declining fertility rate.

articles.headline: Gulf tensions squeeze India’s alcobev sector as input costs soar
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia are severely impacting India's alcoholic beverage industry, with crude, fuel, and packaging material costs (glass bottles up 20%, paper cartons up 100%) soaring. Manufacturers face 12-15% higher costs and potential plant shutdowns due to LNG crunch, urging governments to allow price revisions.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank Hires Law Firms to Review Chairman's Abrupt Exit
articles.summary: HDFC Bank has appointed Wadia Ghandy, Trilegal, and a US firm to review former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's resignation. The review will examine board records and whistleblower letters to assess any concerns about unethical practices or governance issues he raised, as part of a proactive measure.

articles.headline: Sebi Proposes Allowing Gifting of Mutual Fund Investments via PPIs
articles.summary: Sebi plans to permit gifting mutual fund investments using prepaid payment instruments (PPIs) or gift cards. These non-reloadable PPIs will be capped at ₹10,000, valid for one year, and include safeguards like mandatory third-party validation and an investment cap of ₹50,000 per investor per year.

articles.headline: Super Micro Computer Explores India Manufacturing Unit Amid AI Demand
articles.summary: US server maker Super Micro Computer is in talks with Maharashtra, Telangana, and Karnataka to establish a manufacturing unit in India. The company, a major Nvidia partner, has seen its business bolstered by the surging demand for AI infrastructure, driving exploration into the Indian ecosystem.

articles.headline: Swiggy Increases Platform Fee by ₹2.40 per Order After Zomato Hike
articles.summary: Food delivery platform Swiggy raised its platform fee to ₹17.58 per order (including GST) from ₹14.99, following a similar hike by rival Zomato. This increase comes as both companies invest in quick commerce and face new competition like Rapido's 'Ownly', impacting consumers financially.

articles.headline: Top Consumer Goods Companies Maintain Capex Plans Despite Iran War
articles.summary: Leading consumer goods makers, including Maruti Suzuki, Hyundai, Tata Motors, LG, Dixon, AWL Agri Business, and Haier India, will proceed with their capital expenditure plans for FY27. They anticipate the Iran war's impact on input costs will be short-lived, with several planning increased investments.

articles.headline: GSP Crop Science Lists at 2.5% Premium on NSE
articles.summary: Agrochemical company GSP Crop Science listed at ₹328 on NSE, a 2.5% premium to its issue price of ₹320. The stock rose to a high of ₹364 and ended at ₹356.2, valuing the company at ₹1,649.1 crore, after its listing on Tuesday.

articles.headline: CMPDI Initial Offer Subscribed 1.05 Times on Final Day
articles.summary: The IPO of Central Mine Planning & Design Institute (CMPDI) closed with an overall subscription of 1.05 times on its final day of bidding. Qualified Institutional Buyers' portion was subscribed 3.48 times, while Non-Institutional Investors and retail portions were undersubscribed.

articles.headline: Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust Units List at 7% Premium
articles.summary: Units of Raajmarg Infra Investment Trust listed on NSE at ₹107, a 7% premium to the issue price of ₹100 per unit. The ₹6,000-crore InvIT IPO was subscribed 13.74 times, demonstrating strong investor interest in infrastructure assets, closing the day at ₹106.8.

articles.headline: Avenir Investment RSC Acquires Controlling Stake in Sammaan Capital
articles.summary: RBI approved Abu Dhabi's Avenir Investment RSC, owned by International Holding Company PJSC, to acquire a 66.5% controlling stake in Sammaan Capital (formerly Indiabulls Housing Finance). The deal, valued at ₹7,700 crore, is a significant Middle Eastern investment in India's financial services, with RBI setting three conditions.

articles.headline: Zerodha Doubles Brokerage Fee to ₹40 for Some Intraday F&O Trades
articles.summary: Zerodha will double its brokerage fees to ₹40 per order for intraday futures and options (F&O) trades from April 1 if traders do not maintain at least 50% of collateral in cash, as required by Sebi. This move comes amid pressure from the proposed STT hike, aiming to bring discipline.

articles.headline: JP Morgan Appoints Rahul Badhwar as Senior Country Officer for India
articles.summary: JP Morgan, the world's largest bank, appointed Rahul Badhwar as its senior country officer for India. Badhwar joins from HSBC, where he spent three decades in sales and trading roles, including global head of corporate sales for markets and securities services, aiming to drive growth.

articles.headline: Saudi Arabia, Kuwait Pursue Energy Deals Despite Regional Conflict
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia and Kuwait are proceeding with planned multibillion-dollar energy deals despite widening conflict. Kuwait Petroleum is leasing part of its pipeline network, attracting private equity, while Saudi Aramco plans to sell a stake in its oil export terminals business, critical for rerouting shipments.

articles.headline: Indian Brands Adopt Restraint in Advertising Amid Cultural Polarisation
articles.summary: Indian advertisers increasingly avoid overtly progressive or conservative messages in ads, opting for safer narratives. After backlash incidents like Tanishq's 2020 ad, marketers fear alienating diverse consumers in a high-growth market, focusing on scale over cultural positioning, contrasting with US trends.

articles.headline: Brands Re-evaluate Media Buying Amid Transparency Debate with The Trade Desk
articles.summary: A growing dispute between agency networks (Publicis Groupe, WPP, Dentsu) and demand-side platform The Trade Desk over transparency, undisclosed fees, and control is causing brands to re-evaluate their media buying strategies. Brands demand clarity on budget flow and performance accountability in the programmatic ecosystem.

articles.headline: Amazon Ads VP Emphasizes Outcomes Over Budget Splits for Brands
articles.summary: Girish Prabhu, VP of Amazon Ads India, states winning brands focus on clear outcomes and learning loops on the platform, rather than rigid budget splits. He notes that advertising is an integral part of distribution in an intensely competitive marketplace, where AI tools will make low-hanging fruits disappear.

articles.headline: Government Consults Stakeholders on Proposed Digital Competition Bill Framework
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs has initiated discussions with technology companies and industry bodies regarding the draft Digital Competition Bill. The bill aims to introduce ex-ante regulations for large digital platforms to prevent anti-competitive behavior. Tech firms have raised concerns that the move could stifle innovation and complicate compliance for global digital services.

articles.headline: Questions abound on Puch AI's MoU with UP
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh CM Yogi Adityanath's announcement of a ₹25,000 crore MoU with AI startup Puch AI drew criticism over the company's credentials. Invest UP CEO stated it's a non-binding MoU and due diligence is ongoing, with no cost to taxpayers.

articles.headline: AI drives surge in data, cloud, cyber spends despite tight tech budgets
articles.summary: AI-led transformation is boosting spending on data modernization, cloud, and cybersecurity for IT services firms, despite overall tech budget constraints. Accenture and Indian IT firms report increased demand for AI-related work, making previously uneconomic tasks feasible and requiring significant process redesign and skill upgrades.

articles.headline: Lessons from China’s AI plans for data as a national resource
articles.summary: China is building infrastructure to treat data as a national resource for AI, with over 20 government-backed data exchanges trading datasets across industries. This centralized model allows vast economic information to become AI training material, contrasting with US private ecosystems and Europe's privacy frameworks. India, with its public digital infrastructure, faces a similar data organization challenge.

articles.headline: Tech firms pivot to lateral hiring, ready-to-deploy talent in cybercrime
articles.summary: India's IT sector is shifting to lateral hiring over freshers in 2026's first quarter, prioritizing experienced employees with niche skills in AI/ML, cloud, and cybersecurity. Companies like TCS and Infosys are actively recruiting, as demand for immediate capability increases. This leads to higher salaries and intense competition for talent.

articles.headline: Prosus, Accel back 6 deep-tech start-ups under Atoms X LeapTech cohort
articles.summary: Global investors Prosus and Accel unveiled six deep-tech start-ups in their inaugural Atoms X LeapTech cohort, focusing on science-led, long-gestation innovation. These include Praan (air infrastructure), QOSMIC (satellite comms), Dognosis (cancer detection), and Ethereal Exploration Guild (reusable orbital launch vehicles).

articles.headline: Meesho bets on AI voice assistant ‘Vaani’ to onboard next 500 m users
articles.summary: Meesho launched 'Vaani', an AI-powered voice shopping assistant, to target India's digitally hesitant consumers and onboard the next 500 million users. The in-house built assistant uses proprietary speech-to-text, a fine-tuned LLM, and edge computing, showing early traction with 1.5 million users and a 22% conversion rate increase.

articles.headline: Sarvam AI in Talks to Raise $300M, Valuing Firm at $1.2B
articles.summary: Foundational AI startup Sarvam AI is negotiating to raise $250-300 million from Nvidia, HCLTech, and Accel, which would value the company at $1.2 billion. This is over ten times its previous valuation and aims to make it one of India's first AI unicorns, ramping up commercial traction.

articles.headline: Indian AI Startups Face Challenges from West Asia War, Rupee Devaluation
articles.summary: Indian AI startups are facing operational and cost challenges due to the West Asia war, which has stalled expansion plans and impacted logistics for firms like 4baseCare. The rupee's depreciation also adds margin pressure on imported consumables, affecting revenue and operations.

articles.headline: Wispr Flow CEO: Indians Pay for Real Value in AI Services
articles.summary: Tanay Kothari, CEO of voice-focused AI startup Wispr Flow, states India is its second-largest market for paying subscribers. He notes Indians will pay for real value and are particular about it, with 80% opting for annual plans, showing a long-term commitment when trust is built, unlike US users.

articles.headline: Apple to Introduce Advertising in Maps App for Revenue Growth
articles.summary: Apple plans to introduce advertising in its Maps app, allowing retailers and brands to bid for ad slots against search queries (e.g., sushi). This move aims to bolster Apple's services business, which generates over $100 billion annually, similar to Google and Yelp.

articles.headline: Accel, Prosus Partner to Fund 6 Early-Stage Indian Deeptech Startups
articles.summary: Accel and Prosus have partnered through 'Atoms X' to fund six early-stage Indian deeptech startups in air infrastructure, spacetech, cancer diagnostics, fitness, and brain-computer interfaces. Check sizes range from $200,000 to $1 million from each side, with long-term support planned.

articles.headline: SK Hynix to Spend $8 Billion on ASML Chipmaking Tools
articles.summary: SK Hynix will spend 11.9 trillion won ($7.9 billion) on extreme ultraviolet lithography (EUV) chipmaking tools from ASML through 2027. This move deepens its push into next-generation memory, driven by increasing demand for artificial intelligence infrastructure, reinforcing ASML's central role.

articles.headline: Drone Attacks Disrupt AWS Bahrain Region, Company Confirms
articles.summary: Amazon Web Services (AWS) confirmed its Bahrain region service was disrupted by drone attacks, following a Reuters report. The company is helping affected customers migrate to other AWS servers, without specifying the exact location or potential damage done due to ongoing conflict.

articles.headline: Princeton Digital Group to Scale India Data Centre Capacity to 1 Gigawatt
articles.summary: Singapore-based Princeton Digital Group (PDG) plans to acquire multiple small projects in India, adding 210 megawatts in Mumbai and Hyderabad, boosting its total capacity to a gigawatt. This increases its financial commitment to India to $2.5 billion, financed by group equity and debt, driven by hyperscaler demand.

articles.headline: RBI Blocked 61 Million Cyberattacks in Oct-Dec Quarter
articles.summary: The RBI's website faced over 61 million cyberattack attempts in Q3 FY26, all successfully blocked by its security systems. This marks a significant increase from 19 million in Q1 and 31 million in Q2, prompting the use of AI to identify attack patterns and trace their origins.

articles.headline: South Korea's 'K-Games' Emerge as Significant Cultural Export
articles.summary: South Korean-made video games like Stellar Blade and Lies of P are gaining global acclaim, transforming the country's perception of gaming from a social addiction to a "truly authentic export." The government increased support, with $49.7 million from the Korea Creative Content Agency.

articles.headline: NTA Announces New Date for Postponed CUET-UG Exams in Delhi
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency has scheduled the postponed CUET-UG exams for Delhi centers for May 29. The initial test was delayed due to logistical and administrative issues. Approximately 1.5 lakh students will now appear for the standardized test in a single shift. The NTA assured students that new admit cards would be issued and arrangements have been made to ensure a smooth process.

articles.headline: Indian student intake in Canada plunges to 8.1%
articles.summary: Indians formed only 8.1% of international students in Canada last year, a sharp drop from over 51% in 2023. A report to Parliament highlighted the decline.

articles.headline: Canada sees fall in Indian students in 2025
articles.summary: Indian student intake in Canada plunged to 8.1% last year from over 50% in 2023, per an Auditor General report. The immigration department was criticized for not addressing integrity concerns, with 153,000 students potentially non-compliant with study permit conditions.

articles.headline: IIT-Kanpur removing pipes in hostels amid suicides
articles.summary: IIT Kanpur is removing steel pipes from hostel rooms and installing spring-loaded devices in ceiling fans to prevent student suicides. This measure follows nine student suicides at the institute in two years, recommended by an external committee.

articles.headline: Young Lions Finalists Showcase Creative Talent at Cannes Selection
articles.summary: Thirty Gen-Z finalists competed in the Young Lions competition, impressing judges with empathy-led, confident presentations across print, digital, and marketing categories. Winning ideas included reframing instant delivery, combating AI misuse with 'Reality Check', and making pollution palpable through sensory contrasts, securing their Cannes tickets.

articles.headline: Registration Made Mandatory for Chardham Pilgrims to Manage Record Crowds
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand government has made registration compulsory for the Chardham Yatra as pilgrim numbers crossed 25 lakh. Crowds at Yamunotri and Kedarnath have reached record levels, prompting authorities to restrict movement for safety. The government has also deployed additional health teams along the high-altitude routes to assist pilgrims experiencing respiratory or cardiac issues.

articles.headline: Ranveer to apologize, visit Chamundi Hill in mimicry case
articles.summary: Actor Ranveer Singh will file an affidavit apologizing and visit Chamundi Hill, as informed to the high court while hearing his plea to quash an FIR over remarks on a character from 'Kantara Chapter 1'.

articles.headline: Mark Zuckerberg Buys Record $170M Florida Home
articles.summary: Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan reportedly bought a $170 million compound in South Florida, making it the county's most expensive sale. The 1.8-acre property features a 27,889-square-foot limestone mansion with nine bedrooms, adding to their extensive property portfolio.

articles.headline: Chappell Roan Denies Security Made Girl Cry in Brazil
articles.summary: Brazilian soccer star Jorginho accused singer Chappell Roan's security of scolding his 11-year-old stepdaughter, making her cry. Roan denied the incident, stating she didn't see the child and does not hate fans or children, expressing sadness if discomfort was felt after the concert.

articles.headline: Tesseract Show Explores Truth Through Multidimensional Perception
articles.summary: The theatrical show "Tesseract" is praised for its ability to go beyond surface-level illusions, prompting audiences to seek truth beyond immediate perception. It seamlessly weaves a brand's ethos into an immersive experience, making the visible multidimensional and absorbing viewers into its world, unsettling perception.

articles.headline: BRO Completes Strategic All-Weather Tunnel Near LAC in Arunachal Pradesh
articles.summary: The Border Roads Organization has completed a new 500-meter strategic tunnel in Arunachal Pradesh to provide all-weather connectivity to the Line of Actual Control. The project will facilitate the rapid movement of troops and heavy military equipment regardless of snow conditions. This is part of the ongoing infrastructure push to strengthen India's defensive posture along the northern border.

articles.headline: No proposal to shift Jakkur flying training school: Shivakumar
articles.summary: Deputy Chief Minister D K Shivakumar stated the state has no plans to relocate Bengaluru's Jakkur Flying Training School, refuting allegations that the government favored real estate interests.

articles.headline: Govt invokes ECA to expedite gas pipeline laying
articles.summary: The government invoked the Essential Commodities Act (ECA) for a new legal framework, making it mandatory for landowners to grant "right of way" for time-bound laying of gas pipelines. This aims to reduce reliance on imported LPG and simplify procedures for gas infrastructure.

articles.headline: Jal Jeevan Mission 2.0: Beyond pipes and pumps
articles.summary: The extension of Jal Jeevan Mission until 2028 with enhanced outlay marks a new phase, focusing on long-term service sustainability beyond infrastructure. It emphasizes capacity-building for Village Water and Sanitation Committees and inclusive local institutions for reliable water systems.

articles.headline: India must aim to become a global aviation hub
articles.summary: India, as the world's third-largest domestic aviation market, can become a global transit hub by adopting a Dubai/Singapore-like model. This requires seamless airport-airline integration, competitive policies (e.g., zero-tax ATF), world-class infrastructure for quick transfers, and an anchor carrier. Private firms like Adani, GMR, IndiGo, and Air India are key to execution.

articles.headline: Railways set for new refund rules to tackle black tickets
articles.summary: The Railways announced five new reforms, including allowing passengers to change their train boarding station up to 30 minutes before departure and revamping refund rules to curb last-minute speculative bookings. Counter tickets can now be cancelled from any railway station, and the TDR requirement for e-tickets is removed.

articles.headline: Railways to Vet Bid Capacity, Limit Sub-contracting for Big Projects
articles.summary: Indian Railways will assess the bid capacity of contractors for projects above ₹10 crore and introduce a 40% limit on sub-contracting. This aims to ensure quality and timely execution, said Union Railways Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw, citing past instances of bidders subcontracting excessively.

articles.headline: JICA Signs ₹6,100 Crore Loan for Bengaluru Metro Phase 3
articles.summary: Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) signed a loan agreement with India to provide ₹6,100 crore (102,480 million Japanese yen) for the Bengaluru Metro Rail Project Phase 3. The project covers 44.65 km of metro lines and 31 elevated stations, aiming to ease congestion.

articles.headline: National Highway Projects Face 5-8% Cost Increases, Delays Due to West Asia War
articles.summary: India's National Highway projects are experiencing 5-8% cost increases and delays. Input costs for bitumen, steel, cement, and fuel have surged, along with supply-chain disruptions caused by the West Asia war. Industry seeks time extensions, relief from penalties, and compensation for cost escalation.

articles.headline: Iran war hits global trade, from films to wine
articles.summary: The US-Israel war in Iran is causing global economic shocks, leading to inflation and higher borrowing costs. India is highly exposed, with rising fuel prices affecting industries like film (delayed release) and agriculture. WTO warns of risks to global trade.

articles.headline: CAG audit flags gaps in MGNREGA execution in Karnataka
articles.summary: A CAG audit of MGNREGA in Karnataka (2019-24) found procurement violations, payment delays, irregular recruitment, and suspected fraud. Issues included incomplete employment, non-adherence to reservation policy, and payments for non-existent work, pointing to systemic weaknesses.

articles.headline: No dry outs, poor getting LPG on priority, says govt
articles.summary: The government assured adequate petrol and diesel stocks nationwide and priority LPG for 1.1 million Pradhan Mantri Ujjwala Yojana beneficiaries. It's promoting piped natural gas and allocated more commercial LPG, ensuring supplies despite West Asia tensions.

articles.headline: Stronger WTO needed to counter Trump's trade pivot
articles.summary: The article argues for a stronger WTO to combat US unilateralism in trade, which seeks to prioritize American interests through coercive power. It discusses the WTO's legislative crisis, the rise of FTAs, and suggests "variable geometry" in rule-making to maintain trade multilateralism.

articles.headline: EU delays April 15 plan to ban Russian oil imports
articles.summary: The EU Commission delayed its plan to submit a legal proposal to permanently ban Russian oil imports by April 15. The official stated it was not cancelled but postponed due to "current geopolitical developments," though a full phase-out is still planned by late 2027.

articles.headline: India's informal sector had 79.2mn units in 2025
articles.summary: India's informal sector outside agriculture and construction had 79.2 million establishments in 2025, employing 128.1 million workers, with a GVA of ₹19.9 lakh crore, according to NSO data. Its GVA share in India's overall GVA was 6.5%.

articles.headline: Sensex, Nifty rally sharply on Iran 'pause' announcement by US President
articles.summary: Benchmark indices surged Tuesday, with Nifty 50 up 399.75 points (22,912.40) and Sensex up 1,372.06 points (74,068.45). The relief rally followed US President Donald Trump's announcement of a five-day pause on strikes targeting Iranian energy infrastructure, triggering a swift unwinding of geopolitical risk premiums.

articles.headline: China rejects Indian rice shipments, citing GMO presence
articles.summary: China turned away three Indian rice shipments, alleging genetically modified organisms (GMOs) despite prior Chinese agency certification. India, which does not permit commercial GM food crops, views this as a calculated attempt to gain leverage in trade talks. Exporters raised concerns with Apeda and ICAR.

articles.headline: Rupee decline takes real effective exchange rate to 12-year low
articles.summary: The Indian rupee's decline of almost 9% in 12 months, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis and cooling inflation, has pushed its broad-based real effective exchange rate (REER) to a 12-year low. While moderate depreciation boosts exports, continuous fall could harm the domestic economy.

articles.headline: BMW i7, Tata Punch.ev draw same ₹8,000 company-car tax benefit
articles.summary: A revised income tax rule assigns a fixed taxable value of ₹5,000/month (plus ₹3,000 for chauffeur) for company-leased EVs, regardless of price. This makes a ₹2-crore BMW i7 and a ₹12-lakh Tata Punch.ev incur the same ₹8,000/month taxable perquisite, creating significant annual savings for executives.

articles.headline: Private sector capex may fall 16.5% to ₹9.55 lakh crore
articles.summary: Capital expenditure by India's private sector on new asset acquisition is projected to decline by 16.5% to ₹9.55 lakh crore in 2026-27. A government survey indicated that in 2025-26, 60.13% of enterprises undertook capex for income generation, while 42.12% focused on upgrading existing capacity.

articles.headline: Government extends gold import licence validity amid crisis
articles.summary: Amid the West Asian crisis, the government extended the validity of licences for gold imports under quota-based duty concession in a free trade pact with the UAE until June 3. This aims to manage gold supplies and stabilize the market during geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.headline: PSUs and insolvency: Clarity needed on 'sovereign' function bypass
articles.summary: The admission of insolvency proceedings against Cauvery Neeravari Nigam Limited (CNNL) highlights the recurring effort by some public sector enterprises (PSEs) to invoke 'sovereign character' to evade insolvency laws. The author argues that this leaves contractors and lenders exposed and questions whether IBC tribunals should assess sovereign function claims, urging clarity on the issue.

articles.headline: RBI plans ₹1.25 lakh crore year-end Variable Rate Repo boost
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India will conduct two Variable Rate Repo (VRR) auctions aggregating ₹1.25 lakh crore under the Liquidity Adjustment Facility for the financial year-end. This measure aims to provide flexibility to the banking system in managing year-end liquidity mismatches.

articles.headline: Gold extends decline on interest rate expectations
articles.summary: Gold prices fell further on Tuesday due to ongoing West Asia tensions, which have fueled inflation worries and expectations of higher global interest rates. Spot gold decreased by 0.6% to $4,377.93 per ounce, with US gold futures also losing 0.6% to $4,378.80.

articles.headline: Zaid crop coverage drops 2% as paddy acreage trails
articles.summary: Sowing of summer (zaid season) crops reached 42.68 lakh hectares as of March 20, a 2% decline from last year. Paddy sowing is down 3% at 27.86 lh, and nutri/coarse cereals acreage dropped by 12%. Pulses and bajra areas are up, but overall drop in summer acreage persists.

articles.headline: Copper falls after Iran denies talks to end West Asia war
articles.summary: London copper prices slipped, reversing most of the previous session’s relief rally, after Iran denied holding talks with the US to end the West Asia war and launched new missile attacks on Israel. Benchmark three-month copper on the LME fell 1.4% to $11,991.50 a tonne.

articles.headline: Palm oil slides over 1% on weaker rival oils, West Asia caution
articles.summary: Malaysian palm oil futures ended over 1% lower on Tuesday after a long Eid holiday, influenced by weaker rival edible oils and caution from traders regarding the West Asia war. The benchmark June delivery contract slid 72 ringgit to 4,539 ringgit ($1,148.24) a tonne.

articles.headline: Chilli turns hot on strong domestic demand, low crop
articles.summary: Dry red chilli prices are high due to strong domestic demand from North India and masala companies, despite sluggish Chinese demand and ample carry-forward stocks in China. India's chilli crop is down 20-30% on lower acreage, with prices for high-color Byadgi varieties more than doubling.

articles.headline: EU rice millers seek protection amid crisis, potential safeguard duties
articles.summary: European Union (EU) rice millers, led by FERM, are urging the EU to impose safeguard duties on rice imports, including from India, as domestic production faces economic unviability. Rice imports into the EU recently hit 1.7 million tonnes, with significant volumes entering at reduced or zero tariffs.

articles.headline: US trade deal to increase agricultural exports in areas of strength
articles.summary: Union Minister Jitin Prasada announced that the recent trade deal with the US will boost India's agricultural exports in marine products, basmati rice, spices, tea, coffee, oilseeds, and fruits. India protected sensitive agricultural sectors like rice, wheat, and dairy, limiting tariff concessions to quota-based structures.

articles.headline: DeHaat rolls out NHB-supported grape cluster development scheme in Nashik
articles.summary: Agritech firm DeHaat, in partnership with the National Horticulture Board (NHB), launched the Grapes Cluster Development Programme in Nashik. The scheme provides financial aid and end-to-end value chain support to farmers, helping them adopt new, export-oriented grape varieties and improve market access.

articles.headline: Markets Rebound on Peace Hopes, Rupee Gains Amid Gulf War Volatility
articles.summary: Indian equities climbed nearly 2% and the rupee gained against the dollar as traders reacted to a pan-Asian rebound and a US halt in strikes on Iran. This signaled diplomatic efforts, but optimism was tempered by Iran's denial of talks and ongoing attacks, keeping markets volatile.

articles.headline: RBI Asks Banks for West Asia Exposure Details Amid Iran War
articles.summary: The RBI has requested Indian banks to provide details of their direct and indirect exposures to West Asia, including subsidiaries, import/export positions, and NRI loan risks. This aims to assess banking sector risks from the Iran war and plan potential relief measures, as information doesn't reflect in routine reporting.

articles.headline: ONGC Floats $20 Billion Global Tender for Deepwater Rigs
articles.summary: ONGC has issued a tender for $18-20 billion worth of deepwater drilling rigs, its largest-ever capex push under the 'Samudra Manthan' mission to boost energy security. It seeks drillships and semisubmersible rigs for up to five years, requiring mobilisation within 80 days, drawing interest from 12 companies.

articles.headline: Nayara Energy's 35-Day Refinery Shutdown to Cut 8% India Capacity
articles.summary: Rosneft-backed Nayara Energy plans a 35-day shutdown of its 20 million tonnes/year Vadinar refinery from early April, removing 8% of India's refining capacity. This could tighten domestic fuel supply amid West Asia war constraints, following deferred maintenance due to EU sanctions. Nayara has adequate reserves.

articles.headline: Renewable Energy Industry Seeks Removal of Additional VAT on RLNG
articles.summary: The National Solar Energy Federation of India (NSEFI) urged the government to remove additional VAT on regasified LNG (RLNG) supplied from Gujarat to other states. This is due to domestic natural gas supply curtailment and the 9% additional interstate tax burden impacting industries like solar glass manufacturing.

articles.headline: Summer Airfares Soar 10-30% Amid Rising Fuel Surcharges, West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Summer vacation costs are rising significantly, with domestic airfares up 5-10% and short-haul international flights 20-30% dearer. This is due to increased fuel surcharges, higher operating costs linked to the West Asia conflict, and strong holiday demand, impacting package pricing and booking patterns.

articles.headline: Precious Metals May Rebound as Gulf Peace, Dollar Ease
articles.summary: Precious metals are losing sheen amid the West Asia conflict, but a turn in the strengthening dollar could offer a rebound opportunity. Gold has dropped 16% and silver 26% since Feb 27. Analysts project gold towards $5,600 and silver crossing $100 in 1-2 years if tensions ease, making current dips attractive.

articles.headline: Repo Rate Cuts Modestly Reflected in Bank Deposit Rates
articles.summary: Despite a cumulative 125 basis-point cut in the repo rate, outstanding term deposit rates declined by only 42 bps between Jan 2025 and Jan 2026. Banks are reluctant to reprice deposits sharply due to competition and the need to retain funds amidst credit growth outpacing deposit mobilization.

articles.headline: Digital Personal Loans Grow 53% in Value in Q3 FY26
articles.summary: Digital personal loans expanded rapidly in India, with sanction value rising 53% year-on-year in Q3 FY26. Digital NBFCs sanctioned 99 million loans worth ₹1.53 lakh crore, accounting for 78% of total personal loan volumes, while asset quality improved due to stronger underwriting practices.

articles.headline: Russia Delays Budget Rule Pause Amid Higher Oil Prices
articles.summary: Russia postponed adjusting its budget rule, which reduces reliance on energy income, as oil prices surge due to the West Asia war. This delay affects tapping the National Wellbeing Fund and selling assets to finance the deficit, though officials balance it with global economic risk.

articles.headline: Medical student dies by suicide after losing ₹80k in online games
articles.summary: A 21-year-old medical student died by suicide in Bidar, Karnataka, after allegedly losing ₹80,000 in online games. Police suspect distress from the financial losses, with the student having borrowed from friends.

articles.headline: Drugs Controller lens on semaglutide generics use, supply chain
articles.summary: Following the market entry of cheaper semaglutide generics, the DCGI has intensified surveillance on the drug's supply chain and unauthorized sales. Inspections at pharmacies, online platforms, and clinics aim to curb misuse and improper prescription of the GLP-1 drug, used for obesity and Type 2 diabetes. Non-compliance faces strict action.

articles.headline: DCGI Intensifies Surveillance on GLP-1 Weight Loss Drugs
articles.summary: The Drugs Controller General of India (DCGI) is increasing regulatory oversight on GLP-1 weight loss drugs due to concerns over misuse and improper practices. Inspections were carried out across 49 entities, and an advisory was issued against surrogate advertisements and off-label usage, with strict actions promised.

articles.headline: Chemical Costs Surge, Paracetamol Prices Double Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Prices of key chemicals, reagents, and solvents used in APIs have doubled due to supply disruptions from the West Asia war, forcing many manufacturers to halt production. Paracetamol API prices soared from ₹220-240/kg to ₹550-600/kg, squeezing margins across the value chain under government price controls.

articles.headline: Charred body of former MLA's son found in Belagavi
articles.summary: The charred body of Raju Bolashetty, 53, son of a former MLA, was found inside a car in a dense forest in Dharwad, Karnataka. Police suspect assailants killed him and set the car on fire to destroy evidence.

articles.headline: 15 injured in clashes between two SC communities over water
articles.summary: A clash over drinking water access between members of two Scheduled Caste communities in Gadag district, Karnataka, injured 15 people. Police intervened, holding a meeting with elders, who agreed to follow instructions.

articles.headline: Mobile Passport Seva van in Madikeri April 8-10
articles.summary: A Mobile Passport Seva van will operate in Madikeri, Kodagu, from April 8 to 10, offering on-the-spot passport services as part of a Ministry of External Affairs initiative.

articles.headline: Panchayat chief surrenders in porcupine killing case
articles.summary: Vellanad village panchayat president ‘Vellanad’ Sasi surrendered to forest officials in connection with allegedly beating a porcupine to death. He had been absconding since the incident on February 28.

articles.headline: Centre restores FCRA license of Andhra-based NGO RDT
articles.summary: The Centre restored the FCRA license of Rural Development Trust (RDT), an Andhra Pradesh-based NGO, enabling it to resume foreign funding. The license was revoked in September 2025, interrupting welfare initiatives in the drought-prone Rayalaseema region.

articles.headline: Andhra CM directs probe into Amaravati fire accidents
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu ordered a probe into a series of fire accidents at construction sites in Amaravati, focusing on possible conspiracy. A recent fire gutted 22 lots of pipes at an infrastructure company's stockyard.

articles.headline: Ghatkopar hoarding collapse: Probe panel blames IPS officer
