articles.summary: A Maharashtra government panel blamed IPS officer Quaiser Khalid, civic officials, and others for "illegalities" and regulatory violations that led to 17 deaths in the Ghatkopar hoarding collapse. Khalid allegedly permitted an illegal 120x140-feet hoarding unilaterally.

articles.headline: CERN takes antiprotons out for test drive
articles.summary: Scientists at CERN successfully transported antiprotons in a truck as a test, a pioneering and ambitious project to understand fundamental symmetries of nature. This step could enable delivery to researchers abroad, improving measurement accuracy.

articles.headline: NASA to spend $20bn to develop Moon base
articles.summary: NASA plans to invest $20 billion over seven years to develop a base on the Moon's surface. This is a major strategy shift aimed at enabling long-term human habitation on the lunar surface.

articles.headline: Agnikul test-fires pioneering 3D printed booster engine
articles.summary: Chennai-based Agnikul Cosmos successfully test-fired Agnite, a first-of-its-kind, fully 3D printed, more powerful booster-stage engine. The one-metre-long engine, the largest Inconel rocket engine built as a single piece, reduces production time to seven days, supporting faster launch readiness and consistent execution for the space tech start-up.

articles.headline: Missiles hit Tehran, Tel Aviv amid uncertainty over talks
articles.summary: Airstrikes hit Iran and Iranian missiles targeted Israel's Tel Aviv. US President Trump said the US was in talks with Iran to end the war, and delayed bombing threats by five days, citing diplomatic options, but Iran denied talks. The conflict escalated with regional attacks, snarling Strait of Hormuz shipping, raising fuel prices. Pakistan offered to host talks.

articles.headline: Saudi Arabia, UAE take steps towards joining Iran war
articles.summary: Saudi Arabia and UAE are reportedly taking steps to join the Iran war. The WSJ reported Saudi Arabia granted US military access to King Fahd Air Base, and the UAE closed an Iranian-owned hospital and club, signalling potential escalation of the conflict.

articles.headline: No SC status for those who convert: Top court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that converting to Christianity or other religions results in losing Scheduled Caste (SC) status, which depends on professed religion, not birth. Upholding an Andhra Pradesh High Court decision, the court stated loss of status is automatic, terminating constitutional and statutory protections and reservations, as one cannot simultaneously profess Christianity and claim SC status.

articles.headline: Apex court grants one-time pension to SSC women officers
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, using Article 142, ruled that short service commission (SSC) women officers in defence forces, who were denied permanent commission due to unfair evaluations, will receive pension benefits as a "one-time measure," completing 20 years of deemed service.

articles.headline: Kashmiri separatist Andrabi jailed for life in UAPA case
articles.summary: A special NIA court in Delhi sentenced Kashmiri separatist Aasiya Andrabi to life imprisonment for leading a terrorist group conspiring Kashmir's secession from India in a 2018 case. Two accomplices received 30-year sentences. The court noted convicts showed no remorse or repentance, stating leniency would encourage secessionist spirit and pride in their acts.

articles.headline: Must ensure Strait of Hormuz remains open: Modi to Trump
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi told US President Donald Trump that India backs West Asia peace and wants to ensure the Strait of Hormuz remains open. This was their first call since the Iran conflict began on Feb 28, discussing the Gulf situation following US-Israel strikes on Iran, amid rising food and fuel security concerns.

articles.headline: Govt calls all-party meet on West Asia crisis
articles.summary: The government called an all-party meeting on Wednesday to brief lawmakers on the West Asia crisis, with Defence Minister Rajnath Singh chairing and EAM S Jaishankar apprising leaders. PM Modi made statements in both Houses, but Rajya Sabha LoP Mallikarjun Kharge criticized the decision, demanding a House debate instead.

articles.headline: Delhi Budget pivots to capex, junks AAP populism
articles.summary: Delhi's BJP government under CM Rekha Gupta has shifted fiscal priorities from AAP's populism to capital expenditure, proposing ₹30,799.72 crore for 2026-27. This marks a 10% point increase in capex share, aiming to improve living standards and infrastructure, benefiting from synergy with the Union government, and signaling a significant policy turnaround from populist benefits.

articles.headline: Parliament may adjourn early, reconvene after state polls
articles.summary: Parliament's budget session might adjourn early due to five assembly elections next month, but could reconvene for a few days after polls to pass key bills, including women's reservation law amendments, per sources. LoP Kharge criticized the government's decision for an all-party meet over a House debate on the West Asia crisis.

articles.headline: India 'indispensable' for free Indo-Pacific: US's Colby
articles.summary: US Under Secretary of Defense Elbridge Colby stated India is "indispensable" for a free and open Indo-Pacific and a key partner for a favorable balance of power in Asia. He outlined a four-point agenda for cooperation, stressing cooperation despite differences, military power, industrial collaboration, and "strategic candour" as key points.

articles.headline: DMK-led alliance finalizes seat-sharing for Tamil Nadu elections
articles.summary: For the April 23/2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, DMDK reached a seat-sharing agreement with DMK, contesting 10 constituencies. Kamal Haasan's MNM, also in the DMK-led alliance, will not contest. The VCK also signed an agreement with DMK, allotted eight seats (six reserved, two general), an increase from six in 2021.

articles.headline: Brewers warn of price hikes, shortages due to Iran war
articles.summary: Global brewers in India warn of price increases and supply disruptions due to soaring glass bottle costs from a gas shortage caused by the Iran war and shipping delays affecting aluminium imports. India imports 40% of its natural gas from Qatar, making it vulnerable to such geopolitical events.

articles.headline: RCB sold for $1.78 billion, RR for $1.63 billion
articles.summary: An Aditya Birla Group-led consortium bought Royal Challengers Bengaluru for $1.78 billion, and a US-based group led by Kal Somani acquired Rajasthan Royals for $1.63 billion. Both IPL deals need BCCI approval and signal strong commercial appeal for the league with these record valuations.

articles.headline: Manipal Health files IPO papers to raise ₹8,000 crore
articles.summary: Temasek-backed Manipal Health Enterprises filed its DRHP with SEBI for an IPO, comprising a fresh issue of ₹8,000 crore and an OFS of up to 4.32 crore equity shares by promoters/existing investors. Proceeds will repay borrowings, acquire a minority stake in Sahyadri Hospitals, and for general corporate purposes. Pre-IPO placement is also possible.

articles.headline: Euler Motors Raises $47M Equity, $250Cr Debt for EV Expansion
articles.summary: Electric commercial vehicle maker Euler Motors raised ₹437.5 crore ($47 million) in a Series E funding round led by Lightrock, with participation from Hero MotoCorp and Blume Ventures. It also secured an additional ₹250 crore in debt funding from various lenders, planning to invest ₹400 crore for capacity doubling and network expansion over 18 months.

articles.headline: Fertiliser stocks up, adequate for Kharif season
articles.summary: India's fertiliser stocks increased marginally from a week ago, with domestic plants operating at full capacity and inventories higher than last year. Union minister Ashwini Vaishnaw expects "comfortable fertiliser stocks" for the upcoming Kharif season, despite disruptions from the Iran war impacting gas imports.

articles.headline: Navy escorting two LPG carriers out of Gulf of Oman
articles.summary: The Indian Navy is escorting two India-bound LPG carriers, Jag Vasant and Pine Gas, out of the Gulf of Oman after they crossed the Strait of Hormuz. This is amid military tensions due to the America-Israel war with Iran. The vessels, carrying 92,612.59 MT of LPG and 60 Indian seafarers, are headed to Indian ports.

articles.headline: India's Private Sector Growth Slows in March Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: India's private sector output growth slowed to a 3.5-year low in March (HSBC Flash PMI 56.5 from 58.9), with services growth slowest since Jan 2025 and manufacturing since Aug 2021. This slowdown indicates the West Asia war's adverse impact, dampening demand and output, while input costs intensified. Exports, however, remained a record high.

articles.headline: Goldman Sachs Cuts India's 2026 Growth Forecast to 5.9% Due to West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs cut India’s 2026 growth forecast to 5.9% (from 6.5-7%) and CPI inflation to 4.6% (from 4.2%), predicting a 50 bps policy rate hike. This first revision post-West Asia war reflects higher energy prices, longer Strait of Hormuz trade disruption, and changes in oil/gas forecasts, risking imported inflation, currency depreciation, and potential 2026 current account deficits of 2% of GDP or larger for import-dependent India.

articles.headline: Harish Rana, first for passive euthanasia, dies in AIIMS
articles.summary: Harish Rana, 32, India's first person to have life-sustaining treatment withdrawn under a Supreme Court sanction, died at AIIMS on Tuesday. He had been in a permanent vegetative state since 2013, and the ruling established a legal framework for passive euthanasia. His parents donated his corneas and a heart valve.

articles.headline: Railways doubles no-refund window to 8 hours
articles.summary: Indian Railways tightened ticket cancellation rules, doubling the no-refund window from four to eight hours before departure. It also extended percentage-based penalties to cancellations 72 hours out. This aims to curb touts who hold and offload tickets at inflated prices, and prevent misuse.

articles.headline: Senior Maoist Paparao, 17 others surrender in C'garh
articles.summary: Paparao, the last senior Maoist commander in Bastar, surrendered with 17 cadres in Chhattisgarh, marking a "decisive breakthrough" against Left-wing extremism. In Odisha, another wanted Maoist, Sukru, also surrendered with four others, signaling an end to LWE in the region.

articles.headline: Lawyer denies Ricky Rai drove drifting Lamborghini
articles.summary: The lawyer for Ricky Rai, son of Muthappa Rai, denied his client was driving the Lamborghini seen drifting in a viral video in Bengaluru. The car was seized by police. The lawyer claimed a chauffeur was driving and lost control accidentally on a newly paved road.
